title: crispy herbal middle-eastern tofu falafel
description: a crispy and flavorful middle-eastern tofu falafel recipe, great for a sick day. perfect for low-carb diets and easy to make in under 30 minutes.
instructions: mix together the firm tofu, chopped parsley, chopped cilantro, chopped mint, chopped scallions, garlic powder, onion powder, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
add the olive oil, beaten egg, and all-purpose flour to the bowl and mix until well combined.
cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
remove the bowl from the refrigerator and use your hands to shape the mixture into small falafel.
place the falafel on the grill and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until crispy and golden brown.

transfer the falafel to a plate and serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh dill, lemon juice, tahini, water, iceberg lettuce, tomato, pickled turnips, pickled cucumbers, dill sauce, tahini sauce, and tzatziki sauce.
serve the falafel hot, with your favorite dipping sauce.
you can also serve the falafel in a pita with your favorite toppings, such as lettuce, tomato, and tahini sauce.
if you want to make the falafel more crispy, you can coat them in a little bit of cornstarch or panko breadcrumbs before cooking them.
you can also make the falafel ahead of time and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to a day.
when you're ready to cook the falafel, simply place them on the grill and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until crispy and golden brown.
